tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions FCmp optimizations but never explicitly covers vector types. The patch's foldBooleanAndOr generalization applies to vector FCmps as well, but this is not documented in the summary text.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define <2 x i1> @vector_fcmp_reassoc(<2 x double> %a, <2 x double> %b) {
#0:
  %cmp1 = fcmp olt <2 x double> %a, %b
  %cmp2 = fcmp ogt <2 x double> %a, %b
  %and = and <2 x i1> %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret <2 x i1> %and
}
=>
define <2 x i1> @vector_fcmp_reassoc(<2 x double> %a, <2 x double> %b) {
#0:
  ret <2 x i1> { 0, 0 }
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
define i1 @mixed_icmp_fcmp_same_and(double %a, double %b, i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %fcmp = fcmp olt double %a, %b
  %icmp = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %and1 = and i1 %fcmp, %icmp
  %fcmp2 = fcmp ogt double %a, %b
  %retval = and i1 %and1, %fcmp2
  ret i1 %retval
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary states both ICmp and FCmp are handled uniformly, but all existing tests show pure FCmp or pure ICmp chains. Mixed ICmp/FCmp in the same reassociation chain is not explicitly tested or documented.

alive2.src:
define i1 @mixed_icmp_fcmp_same_and(double %a, double %b, i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %fcmp = fcmp olt double %a, %b
  %icmp = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %and1 = and i1 %fcmp, %icmp
  %fcmp2 = fcmp ogt double %a, %b
  %retval = and i1 %and1, %fcmp2
  ret i1 %retval
}

  %icmp = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %and1 = and i1 %fcmp, %icmp
  %fcmp2 = fcmp ogt double %a, %b
  %retval = and i1 %and1, %fcmp2
  ret i1 %retval
}
=>

#0:

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions FastMathFlags are preserved via Builder.setFastMathFlags(), but the specific case of FMF flag intersection (nnan & ninf = no flags) when matching positive zero in foldLogicOfFCmps is not explicitly documented in the summary.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @fcmp_zero_fmf_intersection(double %x, double %y) {
#0:
  %cmp1 = fcmp nnan ord double %x, 0.000000
  %cmp2 = fcmp ninf ord double %y, 0.000000
  %and = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %and
}
=>
define i1 @fcmp_zero_fmf_intersection(double %x, double %y) {
#0:
  %cmp2 = fcmp ninf ord double %y, 0.000000
  ret i1 %cmp2
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
